name: Diode for Alternating Current (DIAC)
shortDescription: A Diode for Alternating Current (DIAC) is a bidirectional trigger diode that conducts abruptly when its breakover voltage is exceeded in either direction, used to trigger TRIACs.
whatIsIt: A DIAC is a bidirectional trigger diode that conducts abruptly when its breakover voltage is exceeded in either direction, used to trigger TRIACs.
whyExists: DIACs provide a symmetric trigger point, improving Triode for Alternating Current (TRIAC) firing symmetry in Alternating Current (AC) controllers.
whereUsed: They are used in lamp dimmers and motor speed controls as TRIAC gate triggers.
